Motion Re: Clarification Of July 6, 2005 Order Re: Bell'S Experts, And For Order That Expert Discovery Be Completed Re: Plaintiff'S Expert Dr. Samuel Hammar And Joint Defense Experts Drs. Sameul Spivak And Charles Mcdonald; Exhibits In Support
Off Calendar Aug-03-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jane M. Weston for defendant and moving party: Bell Industries, Inc.
James N. Sinunu for defendant Duro Dyne Corporation
Chris Andreas for plaintiff and opposing party: Genaro Garcia

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Bell Industries' motion to modify the prior court order is granted in part. The depositions of Dr. Spivak and McDonald may be taken pursuant to the previous order of July 6, 2005: said depositions are to be completed by August 22, 2005. The costs associated with the deposition are to be paid by defendant. The first three hours of expert fees are to be paid by defendant. The parties are to meet and confer as to whether the depositions will be conducted telephonically. If counsel for plaintiff intends to conduct the examination in person, defendant is not responsible for the costs of said travel.

The Court finds good cause to permit the further deposition of Dr. Hammer in light of the subsequent activities of the witness. The deposition is to be completed by August 22, 2005. The allocation of costs and fees shall be pursuant to CCP 2034.430 et seq. The Court denies the request to order Dr. Hammer to produce the file relating to radiological issues and mesothelioma as the evidence submitted in support of the motion does not indicate the witness relied on a particular article or treatise in forming his opinion. ORDER SIGNED. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
